modelIdentification.name: Cyber FOM Module
modelIdentification.releaseRestriction:
Copyright © 2024 by the Simulation Interoperability Standards Organization, Inc. 7901 4th St N, Suite 300-4043, St. Petersburg, FL 33702 All rights reserved.
Reproduction and distribution of this document in whole or in part by any medium is permitted. Appropriate acknowledgement of the source and ownership of the material should be made with any such reproduction and distribution. Please note that this document may be revised periodically. The latest edition will be made available at the SISO website at no charge. The document on the SISO website is considered to be the definitive version. SISO Inc. Board of Directors 7901 4th St N, Suite 300-4043, St. Petersburg, FL 33702
modelIdentification.purpose: The Cyber DEM [SISO-STD-025-2023] represents cyber events and objects in a format independent of simulation interoperability solutions, but is unambiguously translatable to those solutions. It provides the common representation of these cyberspace conditions so they can be transmitted bi-directionally between cyber ranges, cyber simulations, and the test / training environments supported by traditional kinetic simulation. This Cyber FOM [SISO-STD-025.3-2024] is the HLA-specific implementation of the Cyber DEM.
modelIdentification.description: Cyber FOM based on final version of the Cyber DEM

reference.identification:
SISO-REF-072-2024 : Reference for Cyber Modeling & Simulation Study Group, Cyber Data Exchange Model (DEM) Base Objects, Networks, Effects, & Specifications (BONES)
https://www.sisostandards.org/resource/resmgr/reference_documents_/siso-ref-072-2024.pdf
See https://www.sisostandards.org/page/ReferenceDocuments for the latest version.

modelIdentification.other: SAC Approved: 30 September 2024 EXCOM Approved: 14 October 2024

objectClass.name: CyberObject
objectClass.semantics: An entity in cyberspace (physical, logical, or persona layers)

attribute.name: ObjectID
attribute.semantics: A federation unique identifier for the object

attribute.name: Name
attribute.semantics: String identifier for use in user interfaces, visualization, analysis, etc.

attribute.name: Description
attribute.semantics: A short description of the object

attribute.name: RelatedObjects
attribute.semantics: The type of relationship identified between this object and other objects, such as Administer, AdministeredBy, ComponentOf, HasComponent

objectClass.name: Network
objectClass.semantics: A data network including LANs, WANs, tactical radio data networks, cellular data networks, etc.; networks can be composed of other networks, e.g. a WAN is a collection of LANs. CyberObject.Name is the domain name or subnet address range.

attribute.name: Protocol
attribute.semantics: The OSI Model network layer

attribute.name: Mask
attribute.semantics: Used to divide networks into subnets: the format of the mask is dependent upon the networking protocol. IPv4 would use the quad-dotted decimal notation, e.g. 255.255.255.x.

objectClass.name: NetworkLink
objectClass.semantics: A (physical or logical) data link (or bus) between two or more devices

attribute.name: DataLinkProtocol
attribute.semantics: DataLinkProtocol specifies the protocol between network interfaces on this link

attribute.name: Bandwidth
attribute.semantics: Maximum throughput of the link (in bits/second)

attribute.name: Latency
attribute.semantics: Delay between sending and receiving of packets (in milliseconds)

attribute.name: Jitter
attribute.semantics: Duration between individual packets (in milliseconds)

attribute.name: NetworkInterfaces
attribute.semantics: The endpoints of the network link

objectClass.name: PhysicalNetworkLink
objectClass.semantics: A physical data link (or bus) between two or more devices

attribute.name: PhysicalLayer
attribute.semantics: The hardware means of sending and receiving data on a carrier (OSI Model layer 1)

objectClass.name: LogicalNetworkLink
objectClass.semantics: A logical data link (or bus) between two or more devices

objectClass.name: Device
objectClass.semantics: An electronic device capable of operating in cyberspace

attribute.name: DeviceTypes
attribute.semantics: The kind of device (multi-purpose devices would have multiple types)

attribute.name: IsVirtual
attribute.semantics: Is the device virtualized (e.g. a VM)

attribute.name: Role
attribute.semantics: Describes the purpose of the devices that are part of a system or network (e.g. domain controller or system controller)

attribute.name: DeviceIdentifier
attribute.semantics: Device unique identifier, e.g. uniform resource number (URN)

attribute.name: NetworkInterfaces
attribute.semantics: The endpoints of the network link

objectClass.name: Application
objectClass.semantics: A specific instance of a software program running on a device

attribute.name: Version
attribute.semantics: Vendor or developer assigned version

attribute.name: Company
attribute.semantics: Developer/Producer name

objectClass.name: OperatingSystem
objectClass.semantics: The software that supports a computer's basic functions, such as scheduling tasks, executing applications, and controlling peripherals

attribute.name: OSType
attribute.semantics: Type of software supporting a computer's basic functions, such as UNIX-Linux, MicrosoftWindows, Android, AppleiOS

objectClass.name: Service
objectClass.semantics: An application that provides a service to network clients

attribute.name: ServiceType
attribute.semantics: Type of service, such as DNS, Email, Web, Database

attribute.name: Address
attribute.semantics: A service defined address (e.g. web servers have URLs, database servers have connection strings)

objectClass.name: Persona
objectClass.semantics: A user or profile for a person within cyberspace

objectClass.name: Data
objectClass.semantics: Information encapsulated in an instance or collection of file(s), message(s), or record(s) in a database; Data can represent a specific information object or a kind of information, e.g. a specific tactical message or messages that contain SPOTREP information.

attribute.name: Sensitivity
attribute.semantics: Classification or distribution restrictions

attribute.name: DataType
attribute.semantics: Type of the data

attribute.name: Encrypted
attribute.semantics: Encryption type used on the data

attribute.name: Status
attribute.semantics: Status/integrity of the data

attribute.name: Confidentiality
attribute.semantics: 100% means complete confidentiality, 0% means total loss of confidentiality

attribute.name: PacketSize
attribute.semantics: Average size (in bytes) of individual packets

attribute.name: PacketSizeStandardDeviation
attribute.semantics: Variability of packet sizes (0.0 for fixed sized packets)

attribute.name: Frequency
attribute.semantics: Packets per second

attribute.name: IsDuplex
attribute.semantics: True if the communications bidirectional

objectClass.name: System
objectClass.semantics: A system is a collection of CyberObjects (components/subsystems) that work together.

attribute.name: SystemType
attribute.semantics: Type of system, such as SCADA, C2, ICS

interactionClass.name: CyberEvent
interactionClass.semantics: A non-persistent cyber event as opposed to a persistent cyber object

parameter.name: EventID
parameter.semantics: Federation-unique ID for the event to support future actions, e.g. Suspend

parameter.name: Description
parameter.semantics: A human readable description of the event

parameter.name: EventTime
parameter.semantics: Simulation time of the current phase of the CyberEvent

parameter.name: TargetIDs
parameter.semantics: An array of object IDs representing targets

parameter.name: TargetModifiers
parameter.semantics: Key-value pairs providing additional filtering criteria for the target(s)

parameter.name: Phase
parameter.semantics: Execution phase of a Cyber Event, such as Start, Suspend, Resume, End

parameter.name: Duration
parameter.semantics: Length of time (in seconds) the event occurs

parameter.name: ActorIDs
parameter.semantics: A ist of IDs of the perpetrators involved in this Cyber Event

parameter.name: SourceIDs
parameter.semantics: A list of IDs of the simulations that this Cyber Event came from

parameter.name: Payload
parameter.semantics: Contains the details of the event itself (including an indication if the details are inserted/updated/deleted) OR the message after the event, as decided by the federation agreement

parameter.name: RequestAcknowledgement
parameter.semantics: True if the receiver should send a CyberAcknowledge back to the sender

interactionClass.name: CyberEffect
interactionClass.semantics: A change, in or through cyberspace, which is a result or consequence of an action or other cause (effect: www.lexico.com, adapted)

interactionClass.name: Deny
interactionClass.semantics: To prevent access to, operation of, or availability of a target function by a specified level for a specified time, by degrade, disrupt, or destroy (JP3-12)

interactionClass.name: Degrade
interactionClass.semantics: To deny access to, or operation of, a target to a level represented as a percentage of capacity (JP3-12)

parameter.name: IsRandom
parameter.semantics: Whether or not the disruption is uniform or random

parameter.name: Percentage
parameter.semantics: Percentage of degradation (where 100.0 is equivalent to disrupt)

interactionClass.name: LoadRateEffect
interactionClass.semantics: Impact on data upload or download rate.. Percentage (from parent) describes amount of load rate degradation.

parameter.name: RateType
parameter.semantics: Type (direction) of load that is effected

interactionClass.name: DelayEffect
interactionClass.semantics: Number of seconds to delay delivery of data. If Degrade.IsRandom = TRUE, Seconds is randomized for each packet.

parameter.name: Seconds
parameter.semantics: Number of seconds to delay delivery of data

interactionClass.name: JitterEffect
interactionClass.semantics: Time delay variance. If Degrade.IsRandom = TRUE, Milliseconds is randomized for each packet.

parameter.name: Milliseconds
parameter.semantics: Time delay variance

interactionClass.name: CPULoadEffect
interactionClass.semantics: Artificial increase in CPU load. Percentage (from parent) describes amount of CPU usage

interactionClass.name: MemoryUseEffect
interactionClass.semantics: Artificial increase in memory usage. Percentage (from parent) describes amount of memory used.

interactionClass.name: DropEffect
interactionClass.semantics: Packet dropping. Percentage (from parent) describes amount of packets dropped

interactionClass.name: HardwareDegradeEffect
interactionClass.semantics: Degradation, but not destruction of, hardware. Percentage (from parent) describes amount hardware is degraded

parameter.name: DegradeType
parameter.semantics: Type of hardware degradation

interactionClass.name: OtherDegradeEffect
interactionClass.semantics: Generic degradation effect. CyberObject.Description should contain a Human-interpretable or machine-readable description of the effect

interactionClass.name: Destroy
interactionClass.semantics: To completely and irreparably deny access to, or operation of, a target (JP3-12)

interactionClass.name: HardwareDamageEffect
interactionClass.semantics: Physical damage to a device

parameter.name: DamageType
parameter.semantics: Type of damage to the hardware

interactionClass.name: Disrupt
interactionClass.semantics: To completely but temporarily deny access to, or operation of, a target for a period of time (JP3-12)

interactionClass.name: BlockTrafficEffect
interactionClass.semantics: Completely block all traffic over a communication channel

interactionClass.name: Detect
interactionClass.semantics: To discover or discern the existence, presence, or fact of an intrusion into information systems. (MITRE, Characterizing Effects on the Cyber Adversary)

parameter.name: AcquiredInformation
parameter.semantics: Key-value pairs describing information acquired as a result of a Detect Cyber Effect

interactionClass.name: Manipulate
interactionClass.semantics: The effect of controlling or changing information, information systems, and/or networks to create physical denial effects, using deception, decoying, conditioning, spoofing, falsification, and other similar techniques (JP3-12). CyberObject.Description should contain the what and how of the manipulation attack.

interactionClass.name: ManipulationEffect
interactionClass.semantics: Describes the type, effected information/system/network, and/or extent (as a percentage) of the manipulation attack

parameter.name: ManipulationType
parameter.semantics: Type of manipulation

parameter.name: PacketManipulationType
parameter.semantics: Type of manipulation

parameter.name: Percentage
parameter.semantics: Percentage of packets to affect between 0.0 and 100.0

interactionClass.name: CyberAction
interactionClass.semantics: The fact or process of doing something, in or through cyberspace, typically to achieve an aim (action: www.lexico.com, adapted)

interactionClass.name: CyberAttack
interactionClass.semantics: A cyberspace action that creates various direct denial effects in cyberspace (for example, degradation, disruption, or destruction) and manipulation that leads to denial, that is hidden or that manifests in the physical domains (JP 3-12).

parameter.name: MitreSubtechniqueIDs
parameter.semantics: Reference(s) to MITRE's ATT&CK Sub-techniques(s)

interactionClass.name: PhishingAttack
interactionClass.semantics: The fraudulent practice of sending messages purporting to be from reputable sources in order to induce individuals to reveal sensitive information or unknowingly initiate another attack

parameter.name: MessageType
parameter.semantics: Type of the message used in the attack

parameter.name: Header
parameter.semantics: Message header, format is dependent on MessageType (e.g. emails should use IMF (RFC 2822) or MIME; Texts should use SMS or MMS; Chat uses XMPP).

parameter.name: Body
parameter.semantics: The body/content of the message, format is dependent on the MessageType (e.g. emails should use IMF (RFC 2822) or MIME; Texts should use SMS or MMS; Chat uses XMPP).

interactionClass.name: ManipulationAttack
interactionClass.semantics: Controls or changes information, information systems, and/or networks to create physical denial effects, using deception, decoying, conditioning, spoofing, falsification, and other similar techniques (JP3-12). CyberObject.Description should contain the what and how of the manipulation attack.

interactionClass.name: DataExfiltration
interactionClass.semantics:
Data exfiltration is the unauthorized copying, transfer or retrieval of data from a computer or server. Data exfiltration is a malicious activity performed through various different techniques, typically by cybercriminals over the Internet or other network. (source: Techopedia.com)
CyberEvent.Target identifies the data being exfiltrated CyberEvent.ActorID identifies the actor performing the exfiltration

interactionClass.name: CyberDefend
interactionClass.semantics: A cyberspace action taken within protected cyberspace to defeat specific threats that have breached or are threatening to breach the cyberspace security measures and include actions to detect, characterize, counter, and mitigate threats, including malware or the unauthorized activities of users, and to restore the system to a secure configuration (JP3-12)

parameter.name: MitreMitigationIDs
parameter.semantics: Reference(s) to MITRE's ATT&CK Mitigation(s)

interactionClass.name: CyberRecon
interactionClass.semantics: Activities in cyberspace conducted to gather intelligence required to support future OCO or DCO (FM 3-12)

parameter.name: ReconType
parameter.semantics: Type of cyber activity, such as AccountDiscovery, BannerGrabbing, Compliance, conducted to gather intelligence required to support future offensive or defensive cyber operations"

parameter.name: MitreDetectionIDs
parameter.semantics: Reference(s) to MITRE's D3FEND Detection(s)

interactionClass.name: CyberAdmin
interactionClass.semantics: Cyber actions that are administrative in nature (performed by authorized actors)

parameter.name: AdminType
parameter.semantics: Type of cyber action of an administration nature, such as Assessment, Collection, Configuration

interactionClass.name: CyberOrder
interactionClass.semantics: A CyberEvent to capture cyberspace related command and control orders/directives

interactionClass.name: CyberAcknowledge
interactionClass.semantics: Response to a CyberEvent that has requested an acknowledgement

parameter.name: RelatedEventID
parameter.semantics: ID of the Cyber Event being acknowledged

parameter.name: AcknowledgeResponse
parameter.semantics: The response to the related event being acknowledged

simpleData.name: UnsignedInteger32BE
simpleData.semantics: Placeholder for new HLAunsignedInteger32BE coming in HLA 4.

simpleData.name: EventIDType
simpleData.semantics: A federation unique identifier for the event; change to HLAunsignedInteger16BE when HLA 4 is released.

enumeratedData.name: ReconTypeEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: The list of types for recon actions

enumeratedData.name: CyberEventPhaseEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Execution phase of a cyber event

enumeratedData.name: MessageTypeEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Message vector for phishing attacks

enumeratedData.name: PacketManipulationTypeEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Type of effect achieved by a packet manipulation cyber effect

enumeratedData.name: HardwareDegradeTypeEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Type of degradation effect to hardware

enumeratedData.name: RelationshipTypeEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Describes relationships between cyber objects

enumeratedData.name: NetworkProtocolEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Types of network protocols

enumeratedData.name: PhysicalLayerEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Types of networks at the physical layer

enumeratedData.name: DataLinkProtocolEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Types of data link protocols

enumeratedData.name: DeviceTypeEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Electronic device types

enumeratedData.name: OperatingSystemEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Types of operating systems

enumeratedData.name: ServiceTypeEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Types of application services

enumeratedData.name: SensitivityEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Classification, releasability, and sensitivity designations for data; see US DoD 5200.1-PH; US HHS CFR 46 parts 160, 162, and 164; US DOL Guidance on the Protection of Personal Identifiable Information; EU General Data Protection Regulation;

enumeratedData.name: DataTypeEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Types of data

enumeratedData.name: SystemTypeEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Types of systems other than those specified by DeviceType

enumeratedData.name: EncryptionTypeEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Type of encryption used (for data)

enumeratedData.name: AcknowledgeResponseEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Responses for an acknowledgement

enumeratedData.name: ManipulationTypeEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Type of effect achieved by a manipulation cyber effect

enumeratedData.name: AdminTypeEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Actions performed on a system

enumeratedData.name: DataStatusEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Confidentiality, integrity, availability status of data

enumeratedData.name: LoadRateTypeEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Which direction of data transfer is impacted by a load rate cyber effect

enumeratedData.name: HardwareDamageTypeEnum
enumeratedData.semantics: Type of physical damage rendered by a hardware damage cyber effect

arrayData.name: IDArray
arrayData.semantics: Array of object class instance IDs

arrayData.name: Blob
arrayData.semantics: Value of the criterion identified in the key

arrayData.name: MitreID
arrayData.semantics: A (String) ID from the MITRE ATT&CK or D3FEND matrix. Sub-technique IDs are formatted as T####.###. Mitigation IDs are formatted as M####. Detection IDs are formatted as D3-xxxxx.

fixedRecordData.name: KeyValuePair
fixedRecordData.semantics: A keyword (or phrase) and associated value

field.name: Key
field.semantics: Identification of the criterion for additional filtering

field.name: Value
field.semantics: Value of the criterion identified in the key. The datatype is based on the keyword.

fixedRecordData.name: RelatedObjectStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Describes properties about a relationship between objects

field.name: RelatedObjectID
field.semantics: The ID of the related CyberObject

field.name: RelationshipType
field.semantics: Type of relationship identified between this object and other objects, such as Administer, AdministeredBy, ComponentOf, HasComponent

field.name: RelatedObjectPrivileges
field.semantics: List of privileges the object with respect to the related object (e.g. a persona might possess privileges specific to a device or application)

fixedRecordData.name: NetworkInterfaceStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: A network interface (hardware and/or software) that enables a device to connect to a network link. A network interface can exist with or without a link, but not without a device.

field.name: RelatedObjectID
field.semantics: The identifier of the related object (either a Device or NetworkLink)

field.name: Name
field.semantics: The hostname of the device on the network link

field.name: Address
field.semantics: A network specific address (e.g. the IP address or URN)

fixedRecordData.name: TimeStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Simulation time specified in a federation-specific format. Based on the DIS ClockTimeStruct used in the RPR FOM

fixedRecordData.name: ObjectIDType
fixedRecordData.semantics: A federation unique identifier for the object

note.label: cyberdemmodule_3
note.semantics: Installation, configuration, troubleshooting, and maintenance of systems, networks, data, or accounts

note.label: cyberdemmodule_4
note.semantics: Determining deviations from acceptable configurations, enterprise, or local policy; determining level of risk; analysis of operational and technical security controls

note.label: cyberdemmodule_5
note.semantics: Collection of data located on the network or system(s) for the purposes of intelligence, assessment, or planning

note.label: cyberdemmodule_6
note.semantics: Making changes to system, software, security, or network settings

note.label: cyberdemmodule_7
note.semantics: Analyzing systems for compliance with specifications and requirements

note.label: cyberdemmodule_8
note.semantics: Collection, processing, preservation, and/or analysis of computer-related evidence in support of network vulnerability mitigation and/or criminal, fraud, counterintelligence, or law enforcement investigations

note.label: cyberdemmodule_9
note.semantics: Analyze information technology (IT) or cybersecurity events related to systems, networks, and digital evidence

note.label: cyberdemmodule_10
note.semantics: Support, administration, or maintenance necessary to ensure effective and efficient information technology (IT) system performance and security

note.label: cyberdemmodule_12
note.semantics: Execution of all or part of a system to evaluate and verify compliance with specifications or requirements

note.label: cyberdemmodule_16
note.semantics: Resume paused cyber event with changes to parameters

note.label: cyberdemmodule_17
note.semantics: End cyber event regardless of remaining duration

note.label: cyberdemmodule_18
note.semantics: The cyber event is cancelled regardless of it's current phase

note.label: cyberdemmodule_19
note.semantics: IEEE 802.3 networking protocol (based on the Carrier Sense Multiple Access/Collision Detection CSMA/CD protocol), used for connected devices in a wired LAN or WAN

note.label: cyberdemmodule_20
note.semantics: A capability allowing computers, smartphones, or other devices to connect to the internet or communicate with one another wirelessly within a particular area

note.label: cyberdemmodule_22
note.semantics: A particular implementation of the physical layer of the AppleTalk networking system from Apple Computer

note.label: cyberdemmodule_24
note.semantics: IEEE 802.5, a local area network (LAN) topology that sends data in one direction throughout a specified number of locations by using a token

note.label: cyberdemmodule_26
note.semantics: A short-range wireless technology standard that is used for exchanging data between fixed and mobile devices and building personal area networks (PANs)

note.label: cyberdemmodule_28
note.semantics: Data is complete, not damaged or impaired in any way

note.label: cyberdemmodule_29
note.semantics: Data has been modified or accessed without authorization

note.label: cyberdemmodule_31
note.semantics: Data has been altered (by unauthorized means)

note.label: cyberdemmodule_32
note.semantics: Data is encrypted and not accessible by authorized users

note.label: cyberdemmodule_33
note.semantics: Data was deleted and is irretrievable

note.label: cyberdemmodule_36
note.semantics: File with authentication data, e.g. PKI keys, hashed passwords

note.label: cyberdemmodule_37
note.semantics: Data in motion through a communications channel

note.label: cyberdemmodule_38
note.semantics: A system's configuration data (e.g. MS Windows Registry database)

note.label: cyberdemmodule_39
note.semantics: General device with logic bearing components

note.label: cyberdemmodule_41
note.semantics: General purpose computer, e.g. a server or desktop

note.label: cyberdemmodule_42
note.semantics: Computer that is mobile, e.g. a laptop or tablet

note.label: cyberdemmodule_43
note.semantics: Device used to control another device, e.g. a storage device controller

note.label: cyberdemmodule_44
note.semantics: Non-volatile memory device, e.g. thumb drive, solid-state drive, serial advanced technology attachment

note.label: cyberdemmodule_45
note.semantics: Device that reports certain conditions, e.g. environmental, operating status

note.label: cyberdemmodule_46
note.semantics: Device for producing printed output including paper and 3D materials

note.label: cyberdemmodule_47
note.semantics: Device for producing a digital representation of a physical item, e.g. printed material, fingerprint, facial recognition, highway toll readers

note.label: cyberdemmodule_48
note.semantics: Device that performs non-computer communications, e.g. radio, telephone, cellular

note.label: cyberdemmodule_49
note.semantics: Human machine interface. Devices through which the user interacts with a computer system

note.label: cyberdemmodule_50
note.semantics: Software that inspects digital actions, e.g. Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS)

note.label: cyberdemmodule_51
note.semantics: Internet of things. Device that uses internet protocols for communications, command and control

note.label: cyberdemmodule_52
note.semantics: Device that provides security functions to the system

note.label: cyberdemmodule_61
note.semantics: Physical damage to hardware, e.g. overheating

note.label: cyberdemmodule_62
note.semantics: Hard drive is not readable, e.g. zeroized or file allocation table erased

note.label: cyberdemmodule_67
note.semantics: Indicated volatile memory issue with Microsoft OS

note.label: cyberdemmodule_68
note.semantics: Insert random text into user's typing

note.label: cyberdemmodule_69
note.semantics: System will randomly reboot without operator action

note.label: cyberdemmodule_76
note.semantics: Two way non-voice communication through an application hosted on any device

note.label: cyberdemmodule_77
note.semantics: Two way non-voice communication through a cellular device

note.label: cyberdemmodule_78
note.semantics: Data published on a social media platform

note.label: cyberdemmodule_87
note.semantics: Microsoft's OS with a Windowing UI

note.label: cyberdemmodule_88
note.semantics: Apple's OS with a Windowing UI

note.label: cyberdemmodule_89
note.semantics: Digital Equipment Corporation Virtual Memory System

note.label: cyberdemmodule_93
note.semantics: Cisco's OS for routers and switches

note.label: cyberdemmodule_99
note.semantics: Preventing packets from reaching the destination

note.label: cyberdemmodule_110
note.semantics: Identify resources that are available in an IaaS

note.label: cyberdemmodule_111
note.semantics: Identify resources available via compromised credentials

note.label: cyberdemmodule_113
note.semantics: Host-based scan (PICDSS, HIPAA, ISO 27001, NIST SP800.53)

note.label: cyberdemmodule_115
note.semantics: Database enumeration (Boolean-based blind, time-based blind, error based, union query, stacked query, out-of-band), scans to test for vulnerabilities not actual exploiting

note.label: cyberdemmodule_116
note.semantics: Database enumeration - tables, columns, users, privileges, roles

note.label: cyberdemmodule_141
note.semantics: Identify devices connected to a computer system

note.label: cyberdemmodule_148
note.semantics: Identify processes running on the system

note.label: cyberdemmodule_160
note.semantics: Identify operating system version and patch level

note.label: cyberdemmodule_163
note.semantics: Identify primary user / currently logged in user

note.label: cyberdemmodule_164
note.semantics: Identify registered services on the system

note.label: cyberdemmodule_165
note.semantics: Identify system time and time zone

note.label: cyberdemmodule_177
note.semantics: The citing object administers the cited object

note.label: cyberdemmodule_178
note.semantics: The citing object is administered by the cited object

note.label: cyberdemmodule_179
note.semantics: The citing object is a component of the cited object

note.label: cyberdemmodule_180
note.semantics: The citing object has the cited object as a component

note.label: cyberdemmodule_181
note.semantics: The citing object is contained in the cited object

note.label: cyberdemmodule_182
note.semantics: The citing object contains the cited object

note.label: cyberdemmodule_183
note.semantics: The citing object is a provided by the cited object

note.label: cyberdemmodule_184
note.semantics: The citing object provides the cited object

note.label: cyberdemmodule_185
note.semantics: The citing object resides on the cited object

note.label: cyberdemmodule_186
note.semantics: The citing object has resident of the cited object

note.label: cyberdemmodule_188
note.semantics: Public disclosure would damage national security

note.label: cyberdemmodule_190
note.semantics: Public disclosure would cause serious damage to national security

note.label: cyberdemmodule_191
note.semantics: Secret / restricted to country of source

note.label: cyberdemmodule_192
note.semantics: Top secret, unauthorized disclosure would cause exceptionally grave damage to national security

note.label: cyberdemmodule_197
note.semantics: Top Secret documents managed by a COSMIC registry.

note.label: cyberdemmodule_199
note.semantics: Information a company wishes to keep confidential (e.g. trade secrets)

note.label: cyberdemmodule_201
note.semantics: Health information portability and accountability act

note.label: cyberdemmodule_205
note.semantics: Service that transmits and receives electronic mail messages (typically using SMTP)

note.label: cyberdemmodule_206
note.semantics: Service that hosts web pages and provides them via HTTP/HTTPS

note.label: cyberdemmodule_207
note.semantics: Service that stores and manages data (e.g. RDBMS)

note.label: cyberdemmodule_208
note.semantics: Service that provides files to remote devices on a network

note.label: cyberdemmodule_209
note.semantics: Service that allows users to exchange short messages (e.g. IRC)

note.label: cyberdemmodule_210
note.semantics: Service that allows users to share information and collaborate about a particular topic

note.label: cyberdemmodule_211
note.semantics: Website and/or application that enables users to create and share content or to participate in social networking

note.label: cyberdemmodule_212
note.semantics: Service that packages libraries, frameworks, and applications into an isolated execution environment utilizing shared resources on a host platform

note.label: cyberdemmodule_213
note.semantics: Technology that simulates physical hardware and represents it as a separate virtual machine (VM)

note.label: cyberdemmodule_214
note.semantics: Network protocol for clock synchronization between connected devices (e.g. NTP)

note.label: cyberdemmodule_215
note.semantics: Non-DeviceType system other than SCADA, CT, or ICS
